subject: Mathematics
title: The Infinity Symbol
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Infinity Symbol usable.

hook.text: The sideways figure-eight has been the symbol for infinity since 1655. But why that shape?

buildup.text: John Wallis introduced it in his work on conic sections. He never explained his choice.

discovery.text: Some scholars think it came from the Roman numeral for 1,000 (CIƆ), which resembled a lemniscate.

twist.text: Others suggest the Ouroboros—a snake eating its own tail—inspired the looping shape.

climax.text: Whatever its origin, the lemniscate is now one of the most recognized symbols in all of science.

punchline.text: A mysterious loop became math's most famous symbol.

quiz.question: Who introduced the modern infinity symbol?
